boat.full_description: The instruction manual for the Iain Oughtred, ‘Guillemot’, design no. 54 states: “A strong, stable and very capable boat along the lines of a 19th century ship’s boat or large yacht tender. The Guillemot has been designed for owners who need a more burdensome hull with flatter floors, firmer bilges and higher freeboard. She will not be as light and fast, but is a lot more steady in the water and less ‘flighty’ than the Acorn Skiff. She is finely modelled and performs very well indeed for a boat of this type, whether rowed or under sail. She can happily take a light outboard as well.”
boat.name: Cachalette

ownerships.note: please check this is not a namesake

boat.short_description: An Iain Oughtred 'Guillemot' design, built by her current owner high up on a Derbyshire hillside, 'Cachalette' was launched 2010 on Carsington Water. In 2021 she was moved to the River Deben.
